channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Middle East latest: EU urges Israel to accept ceasefire deal with Hezbollah
item.description: The European Union’s top diplomat said Tuesday there were “no excuses” for Israel to refuse to implement a ceasefire with the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, saying all its security concerns had been addressed in the U.S.-French-brokered deal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden administration will loan $6.6 billion to Rivian to build Georgia factory that automaker paused
item.description: President Joe Biden’s administration will loan $6.6 billion to Rivian Automotive to build a Georgia factory.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Amy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Data exposes dangerous impact of Dems' decision to put Americans last and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What Black Friday's history tells us about holiday shopping in 2024
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The holiday shopping season is about to reach full speed with Black Friday, which kicks off the post-Thanksgiving retail rush later this week.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A violent showdown in Pakistan's capital pits the government against Imran Khan supporters
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Thousands of people are in the heart of Pakistan’s capital to demand the release of imprisoned former premier Imran Khan. He has been behind bars since August 2023 on charges that he and his supporters say are politically motivated.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden proposes Medicare and Medicaid cover costly weight-loss drugs for millions of obese Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Millions of Americans with obesity would be eligible to have popular weight-loss drugs like Wegovy or Ozempic covered by Medicare or Medicaid under a new rule the Biden administration proposed Tuesday morning. The costly proposal from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ukraine says Russian attack sets a new record for the number of drones used
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched 188 drones against most regions of Ukraine in a nighttime blitz, the Ukrainian air force said Tuesday, describing it as a record number of drones deployed in a single attack.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: White Florida woman sentenced to 25 years in prison for shooting Black neighbor in lengthy dispute
item.description: A white Florida woman who fatally shot a Black neighbor through her front door during an ongoing dispute over the neighbor’s boisterous children was sentenced Monday to 25 years in prison for her manslaughter conviction. Susan Lorincz, 60, was convicted in August of killing Ajike “A.J.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top EU diplomat says Israel has 'no excuses' to refuse a ceasefire with Hezbollah
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — The European Union’s top diplomat said Tuesday there were “no excuses” for Israel to refuse to accept a ceasefire with the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, saying all its security concerns had been addressed in the U.S.-French-brokered deal.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Automaker spying on drivers, reporting data to insurance companies
item.description: Since April, seven class action lawsuits have been filed against General Motors, OnStar, and data brokers LexisNexis and Verisk Analytics. The suits accuse GM of partnering with data brokers to collect driving data without proper consent using OnStar Smart Driver technology.
item.dc:creator: Mike Colombo, NewsNation

item.title: The Onion's bid for Alex Jones' Infowars hangs in the balance as judge orders new hearing
item.description: A bankruptcy judge on Monday delayed a hearing in conspiracy theorist Alex Jones’ effort to stop the satirical news outlet The Onion from buying Infowars, keeping the auction sale up in the air for at least another few weeks.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Couple charged in ring suspected of stealing $1 million in Lululemon clothes
item.description: A Connecticut couple has been charged in Minnesota with being part of a shoplifting ring suspected of stealing around $1 million in goods across the country.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Walmart ordered to pay former truck driver $35M after jury finds fraud claims false
item.description: A jury in California found Walmart defamed a truck driver with false claims of workers' compensation fraud and will have to pay the former worker more than $34 million in damages.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bird flu detected in raw milk from California, state officials warn
item.description: The avian influenza virus has been detected in a retail sample of raw milk from a California-based raw dairy farm, California health officials confirmed Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Isaiah Varela, WGN 9

item.title: Jack Smith files to drop Jan. 6 charges against Donald Trump
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith has filed a motion to drop all four felony charges against President-elect Donald Trump in connection with his effort to overturn his 2020 presidential election in the lead-up to the deadly Jan. 6 attack on the U.S Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly and Ken Dilanian, NBC News, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Time running out for Senate to pass Social Security Fairness Act
item.description: The House recently passed a Social Security bill but the Senate is running out of time to get it over the finish line.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 1 dead after DHL cargo plane crashes in Lithuania; officials 'cannot rule out' terrorism
item.description: Lithuanian officials said they were investigating the cause of the crash, and "cannot rule out" terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Siarhei Satsiuk, WGN 9

item.title: Biden pardons turkeys Peach and Blossom for his last Thanksgiving event
item.description: President Joe Biden on Monday pardoned the turkeys Peach and Blossom for the traditional presidential pardon ahead of Thanksgiving, which marks the start of Biden’s last holiday season at the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Macy's says employee hid up to $154 million in expenses
item.description: The company added that the person behind the conduct is no longer an employee and that the investigation didn't identify involvement by any other worker.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Airport workers strike in Charlotte
item.description: Employees at the Charlotte Douglas International Airport have gone on strike over wages during what’s expected to be the busiest travel week of the year with the Thanksgiving holiday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Migrant murders put American communities on edge as over 1.4 million avoid deportation with shady tactics
item.description: 1.4 million immigrants who have court orders to leave the country still reside in the U.S.. An immigration researcher says it took "decades of bad policy" to get to this point.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What's behind the doors of this Missouri cave? A former roller rink, concert stage and more
item.description: Adventure awaits at every turn of one captivating cave in suburban St. Louis, now modernized into a quirky and nostalgic getaway with a storied legacy.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, KRON4

item.title: Remains of Pennsylvania teen at center of 1973 cold case identified as investigation continues
item.description: Ruth Elizabeth Brenneman was last seen leaving for school more than 50 years ago. Now, her remains have been identified as the investigation into her disappearance continues.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KRON4

item.title: Earth bids farewell to its temporary 'mini moon' that is possibly a chunk of our actual moon
item.description: The harmless space rock will peel away on Monday, overcome by the stronger tug of the sun’s gravity. But it will zip closer for a quick visit in January.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Verstappen captures 4th F1 championship after Mercedes sweep of Las Vegas Grand Prix
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Asked how he planned to celebrate his fourth consecutive Formula 1 championship, Max Verstappen did a quick scan of the room searching for a drop of alcohol. “Well, I ran out of beer, so I don't know,” he said. “Maybe another one, and then maybe I switch to something else.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man humiliated on Southwest flight wants an apology from airline
item.description: A Southwest email responding to John Kerrigan's complaint uses phrases such as "we regret your disappointment," but the word "sorry" is nowhere to be found.
item.dc:creator: Chris Horne, KTLA 5

item.title: Florida knocks No. 9 Ole Miss out of College Football Playoff contention, 24-17 in the Swamp
item.description: Coach Billy Napier and his team did something no one at Florida had done in nearly two decades. It could be the turning point for a once-proud program that clearly slipped in recent years.
item.dc:creator: MARK LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the people Trump has picked for key positions so far
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is filling key posts in his second administration, and it's shaping up much differently than his first. He's prioritizing loyalists for top jobs.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hyundai, Kia recall over 200K electric cars over power issues
item.description: Hyundai’s recall encompasses over 145,000 cars, including some IONIQ 5 and IONIQ 6 EVs along with Genesis GV60, Genesis GV70 and Genesis G80 models.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Officer kills pet dog mistaken for a coyote in Massachusetts town. The owner says it was unnecessary
item.description: An animal control officer shot and killed a pet dog in a Massachusetts town after mistaking it for a coyote in an incident that has the animal's owner fuming, but that local police are describing as a sad mix-up.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump expected to name investor Scott Bessent as Treasury secretary pick
item.description: Bessent, founder and CEO of hedge fund Key Square Group, served as an economic adviser to the Trump campaign, often appearing on financial talk shows to argue in favor of the president-elect's economic policies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, Fox 5

item.title: Trump may move Space Command from Colorado to Alabama
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to move U.S. Space Command from Colorado Springs, Colorado to Huntsville, Alabama.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, ABC 4

item.title: No ball at Tropicana Field in 2026 after Milton; new ballpark deal dies: Tampa Bay Rays
item.description: The St. Petersburg City Council has voted to spend over $23 million to repair Tropicana Field after Hurricane Milton destroyed the roof on the home of the Tampa Bay Rays.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, ABC 4

item.title: President of Atlanta's historically Black Spelman College steps down after leave of absence
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Spelman College's president won't be coming back from her leave of absence and is stepping down permanently, the historically Black women's college announced Thursday. The college, which has more than 3,000 students, hasn't said why Dr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Passengers duct tape unruly man who tried to open door on American Airlines plane
item.description: A group of passengers aboard a Dallas-bound flight restrained a man with duct tape after he claimed to be the "captain" and attempted to open the cabin door of an American Airlines plane midair, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, ABC 4

item.title: Emperor penguin who swam to Australia released 20 days after waddling onto beach
item.description: The only emperor penguin known to have swum from Antarctica to Australia was released at sea 20 days after he waddled ashore on a popular tourist beach
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin man who faked his own kayaking death fled the country and won't come home, officials say
item.description: Borgwardt, 45, was reported missing on Aug. 12 after he failed to return home from kayaking on Big Green Lake, the Green Lake County Sheriff’s Office said at the time.
item.dc:creator: Ben Newhouse, WGN 9
